question: Is a yellow school bus clearly visible in the composite image?
Does the inserted bus retain key visual characteristics of the reference object, specifically the yellow body and 'SCHOOL BUS' text?
Is the bus positioned in the lane immediately adjacent to the bus stop shelter (located in the bottom-left quadrant of the intersection)?
Is the bus positioned closer to the curb/sidewalk side of the lane, visually suggesting the action of 'pulling over'?
Is the bus oriented parallel to the lane markings and facing towards the intersection, consistent with the traffic flow indicated by the stop line?
Has the bus been correctly transformed from the reference side-view to a top-down/aerial perspective (roof visible) to match the background scene?
Is the scale of the bus realistic relative to the environment (i.e., fitting within the lane width and appearing larger than the silver sedans)?
Does the bus cast a visible shadow on the road surface?
Is the direction of the bus's shadow consistent with the scene's existing shadows (casting towards the upper-right, matching the trees and gas station)?
Does the lighting brightness and color temperature of the bus match the warm, bright midday conditions of the background?
Does the bus appear firmly grounded on the road with appropriate contact shadows near the wheels, rather than floating?
Are the edges of the bus blended naturally into the asphalt without visible white halos, jagged pixelation, or cut-out artifacts?
Is the bus free from unnatural geometric distortions (e.g., warped body shape, melted windows)?
Is the bus stop shelter in the bottom-left corner preserved and visible (not overwritten or distorted by the bus insertion)?
Are the surrounding road markings and other vehicles in the scene preserved without distortion?
